repo_notes.content: The project ports the McBopomofo Input Method for macOS to JavaScript supported platforms.

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce McBopomofoWeb, explaining what it is, its purpose as a multi-platform Bopomofo input method, and high-level system capabilities

pages.title: System Architecture
pages.purpose: Present the high-level architecture showing how the TypeScript core is compiled into multiple platform-specific implementations and how major subsystems relate

pages.title: Core IME Engine
pages.purpose: Overview of the core input method engine components, introducing the main classes and their responsibilities

page_notes.content: Note that the input and output are both abstracted. There is an abstract layer for Key events for web browser and TSF key events. The output is also an interface and the core IME engine does not manipulate the UI and output directly.

pages.title: Input Processing Pipeline
pages.purpose: Detail the flow from keyboard input to character output, covering InputController, KeyHandler, and the event-driven architecture

page_notes.content: In the TypeScript code, we do not use create the JavaScript dictionary object directly but call JSON.parse to use the native JSON parser but not the JavaScript parser to load the data. It can improve the loading performance.

Explain where the input data comes from. We do not build data in the project but convert the input data from an upstream project, McBopomofo, and convert them into JSON format to let webpack to pack the data into a single JavaScript package.

pages.title: Phonetic Processing and Keyboard Layouts
pages.purpose: Explain BopomofoReadingBuffer, BopomofoSyllable, keyboard layouts (Standard, ETen, Hsu, etc.), and Hanyu Pinyin support

pages.title: State Machine and Input States
pages.purpose: Document the InputState hierarchy (Empty, Inputting, ChoosingCandidate, Marking, etc.) and state transition logic

pages.title: Language Model and Reading Grid
pages.purpose: Explain WebLanguageModel, ReadingGrid, Viterbi algorithm for candidate path finding, and dictionary structure

pages.title: Dictionary Services and Character Information
pages.purpose: Cover dictionary lookup services, character information display, and external dictionary integration

pages.title: User Learning and Personalization
pages.purpose: Explain UserOverrideModel, user phrase management, excluded phrases, and how the system learns from user selections

pages.title: Candidate Selection and Display
pages.purpose: Detail CandidateController, pagination logic, candidate window management, and selection methods

pages.title: Special Input Features
pages.purpose: Document special features like Big5 code input, number input, date macros, marking mode, and Ctrl+Enter options

pages.title: Text Conversion Services
pages.purpose: Overview of the Service module and its role in providing text transformation capabilities beyond character input

pages.title: Service Layer Architecture
pages.purpose: Explain the Service class design, its integration with language model, and the convertText method pattern

pages.title: Bopomofo-Braille Conversion System
pages.purpose: Detail the bidirectional Bopomofo-Braille conversion, BopomofoBrailleConverter, state machine, and token processing

pages.title: Text Format Conversions
pages.purpose: Cover conversions to HTML Ruby, Bopomofo readings, raw readings, Hanyu Pinyin, and their use cases

pages.title: Platform Implementations
pages.purpose: Overview of how the core engine is adapted to different platforms through platform-specific implementations

pages.title: Platform Adapter Pattern and InputUI Interface
pages.purpose: Explain the InputUI abstraction and how different platforms implement it for their specific UI systems

pages.title: ChromeOS Extension Implementation
pages.purpose: Overview of the Chrome extension architecture, manifest structure, and IME components

pages.title: ChromeOS IME Integration
pages.purpose: Detail chrome.input.ime API integration, event handlers, service worker, and content script

pages.title: ChromeOS Settings and Storage
pages.purpose: Explain chrome.storage.sync usage, settings management, localization, and LargeSync for user phrases

pages.title: ChromeOS Context Menus and Notifications
pages.purpose: Cover context menu integration, commands, notification system, and mode toggles

pages.title: Windows PIME Implementation
pages.purpose: Overview of PIME integration, architecture differences from Chrome, and Windows-specific features

pages.title: PIME Request/Response Protocol
pages.purpose: Detail the request/response communication pattern, method handlers, and UI state management

pages.title: PIME Configuration and File Management
pages.purpose: Explain file-based settings storage, user phrase files, file watching, and options UI

pages.title: Web Example Application
pages.purpose: Document the web demo, its features, IndexedDB storage, text transformation tools, and UI architecture

pages.title: MCP Server for AI Integration
pages.purpose: Explain the Model Context Protocol server, its tools, stdio transport, and integration with Claude/Gemini

pages.title: Microsoft Word Add-in
pages.purpose: Cover the Office Add-in implementation, build pipeline, and integration with Word

pages.title: Build System and Deployment
pages.purpose: Overview of the multi-platform build strategy and how a single codebase produces five different distributions

pages.title: Project Structure and Dependencies
pages.purpose: Detail package.json, dependencies, npm scripts, and module organization

pages.title: Multi-Platform Build Strategy
pages.purpose: Explain webpack configurations for each platform, build:chromeos, build:pime, build:mcp, and shared code strategies
